questions.question: In the figure, lines AB and CD intersect at O. If ∠AOC = 58°, then the vertically opposite angle ∠BOD = ____°.
questions.method_hint: Vertically opposite angles formed by two intersecting lines are equal.
questions.explanation: ∠AOC and ∠BOD are vertically opposite angles, so ∠BOD = 58°.

questions.question: In the figure, POQ is a straight line. If ∠POR = 47°, then ∠ROQ = ____°.
questions.method_hint: Angles forming a linear pair on a straight line add up to 180°.

questions.question: In the figure, ∠A and ∠B form a linear pair. If ∠A = 112°, then ∠B = ____°.
questions.method_hint: A linear pair sums to 180°.

questions.question: In the figure, two lines intersect. If one angle is 39°, then its vertically opposite angle is ____°.
questions.method_hint: Vertically opposite angles are equal.

questions.question: In the figure, lines l and m intersect at P. If ∠1 = 90°, then ∠1 is a ____° angle.
questions.method_hint: An angle of 90° is called a right angle.

questions.question: In the figure, lines AB and CD intersect at O. If ∠AOC = 36°, then ∠AOD = ____°.
questions.method_hint: Adjacent angles on a straight line form a linear pair and sum to 180°.

questions.question: In the figure, OP ⊥ OQ. The measure of ∠POQ is ____°.
questions.method_hint: Perpendicular lines intersect at 90°.

questions.question: In the figure, lines intersect and ∠x = 71°. The angle adjacent to ∠x on the straight line is ____°.
questions.method_hint: Adjacent angles on a straight line add to 180°.

questions.question: In the figure, l ∥ m and t is a transversal. If ∠1 = 65° (corresponding angles), then ∠5 = ____°.
questions.method_hint: Corresponding angles are equal when lines are parallel.

questions.question: In the figure, l ∥ m and line t is a transversal. If ∠3 = 52° (alternate interior angles), then ∠6 = ____°.
questions.method_hint: Alternate interior angles are equal for parallel lines.

questions.question: In the figure, l ∥ m. If ∠4 = 118° (co-interior angles), then ∠5 = ____°.
questions.method_hint: Co-interior angles are supplementary when lines are parallel.

questions.question: In the figure, l ∥ m and t is a transversal. If ∠2 = 44° (corresponding angles), then ∠6 = ____°.
questions.method_hint: Corresponding angles are equal when the lines are parallel.

questions.question: In the figure, AB ∥ CD and EF is a transversal. If ∠AEF = 73°, then the alternate interior angle ∠EFD = ____°.
questions.method_hint: Alternate interior angles are equal between parallel lines.

questions.question: In the figure, l ∥ m. The co-interior angles on one side of transversal t are 105° and x. Then x = ____°.
questions.method_hint: Co-interior angles between parallel lines add up to 180°.

questions.question: In the figure, l ∥ m. If an acute angle formed by the transversal is 28°, then another acute angle in the alternate interior position is ____°.
questions.method_hint: Alternate interior angles are equal when lines are parallel.
questions.explanation: The alternate interior acute angle is 28°.

questions.question: In the figure, l ∥ m. If ∠7 = 132°, then the co-interior angle ∠4 on the same side of the transversal is ____°.
questions.method_hint: Co-interior angles are supplementary for parallel lines.

questions.question: In the figure, lines l and m are cut by transversal t. If corresponding angles ∠1 and ∠5 are both 81°, then ∠1 − ∠5 = ____°.
questions.method_hint: Equal corresponding angles mean the lines are parallel.

questions.question: In the figure, transversal t cuts lines l and m. If alternate interior angles are 54° and x, and the lines are parallel, then x = ____°.
questions.method_hint: Parallel lines give equal alternate interior angles.

questions.question: In the figure, corresponding angles measure 76° and 76°. The common measure is ____°.
questions.method_hint: Equal corresponding angles indicate parallel lines.

questions.question: In the figure, co-interior angles are 111° and y. If the lines are parallel, then y = ____°.
questions.method_hint: Co-interior angles are supplementary when lines are parallel.

questions.question: In the figure, ray OX stands on line PQ. If ∠POX = 61°, then ∠XOQ = ____°.
questions.method_hint: Angles on a straight line sum to 180°.

questions.question: In the figure, lines AB and CD intersect at O. If ∠BOD = 127°, then ∠AOC = ____°.
questions.method_hint: Vertically opposite angles are equal.

questions.question: In the figure, l ∥ m and t is a transversal. If ∠8 = 57°, then the corresponding angle ∠4 = ____°.
questions.method_hint: Corresponding angles are equal for parallel lines.

questions.question: In the figure, l ∥ m. Co-interior angles are (2x + 20)° and (3x − 10)°. Then x = ____.
questions.method_hint: Co-interior angles sum to 180°.

questions.question: In the figure, alternate exterior angles are 49° and 49°. The lines are parallel. (Answer 1 for yes, 0 for no.)
questions.method_hint: Equal alternate exterior angles imply parallel lines.
questions.explanation: Both angles are 49°, so the lines are parallel. Answer: 1.

questions.question: In the figure, corresponding angles are (4x − 12)° and (2x + 18)°. For parallel lines, x = ____.
questions.method_hint: Set corresponding angles equal.

questions.question: In the figure, alternate interior angles are (5y − 20)° and (3y + 10)°. For parallel lines, y = ____.
questions.method_hint: Alternate interior angles must be equal.

questions.question: In the figure, co-interior angles are (2p + 35)° and (3p − 5)°. For parallel lines, p = ____.
questions.method_hint: Co-interior angles sum to 180°.

questions.question: In the figure, corresponding angles are (6m − 50)° and (4m + 10)°. For parallel lines, m = ____.
questions.method_hint: Equal corresponding angles give a linear equation.

questions.question: In the figure, alternate interior angles are (7n + 5)° and (5n + 29)°. For parallel lines, n = ____.
questions.method_hint: Set alternate interior angles equal for parallel lines.
